page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The server is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. It also includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in single-core operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all rank in the top 10%. Multi-threaded performance is also strong, with the passmark CPU score and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks fall into the average tier.

This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and large-scale caching systems. While it demonstrates strong capabilities in small-model LLM inference, such as text generation for a 135M model, it exhibits a clear tradeoff when handling larger models. LLM text generation for a 70B model falls into the bottom 10%, indicating that CPU-based large language model inference is a significant bottleneck. The high memory density and substantial local storage make it an efficient choice for enterprise databases and memory-bound applications rather than heavy parallel computation or large-scale AI inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. It features a substantial 512.0 GB of RAM and 3780 GB of local storage, making it highly capable for memory-intensive applications. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong multi-threaded decompression and Passmark CPU scores. However, memory bandwidth and multi-core stress-ng performance remain in the average tier. A key tradeoff is its limited capability for large-scale AI workloads, as text generation for a 70B LLM model falls into the bottom 10%. This server is best suited for large databases, memory-bound enterprise applications, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ads_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz
512.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Suited for large databases, memory-bound enterprise apps, and web serving
Tradeoff in large-scale LLM inference with poor 70B model text generation
